reforms.description: The Australian government aims to enhance innovation in public services by opening up procurement to more bidders and further developing digital government services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging competition in public service procurement can lead to more innovative solutions and improved service delivery, enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was part of the broader National Innovation and Science Agenda announced in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in how public services are procured, aiming to foster innovation and improve service quality.
reforms.source_quote: Encourage more innovation in public services by opening up procurement to more bidders and further development of digital government services.

reforms.description: The government plans to make the R&D Tax Incentive more effective by combining an eligibility threshold with an increase in the expenditure cap.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the R&D Tax Incentive, the government aims to stimulate private sector investment in research and development, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was proposed in the 2016-17 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to improve the effectiveness of a key instrument for promoting R&D in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: Make the R&D Tax Incentive more effective, for instance by combining an eligibility threshold with an increase in the expenditure cap.

reforms.description: The National Innovation and Science Agenda (NISA) was announced to enhance Australia's innovative capacity through various initiatives, including improved access to finance for startups and increased collaboration between universities and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering entrepreneurship and collaboration, NISA aims to stimulate innovation and economic growth, enhancing productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have implemented, or are on track to implement, all measures in the first wave of NISA.
reforms.importance_rationale: NISA represents a comprehensive strategy to enhance innovation across multiple sectors, addressing key barriers and promoting collaboration, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The National Innovation and Science Agenda (NISA) was announced on the 7th of December, 2015.

reforms.description: A new simplified funding system for university research began operating in early 2017, where federal block grants are determined solely by income from competitive grants and other sources, now given equal weight. This reform aims to increase universities' incentives to collaborate and make government competitive grants more responsive to applicants' needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning funding with collaboration incentives, the reform is expected to enhance research quality and relevance, ultimately benefiting economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The new funding system has been operational since early 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how university research is funded, promoting collaboration and responsiveness, which are critical for enhancing innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: A new simplified funding system for university research began operating in early 2017, in which federal block grants for research are determined solely by income from two categories, competitive grants and other sources.

reforms.description: The CSIRO Innovation Fund was launched at the end of 2016 to support co-investment in new spin-off and start-up companies created by CSIRO and other research institutions and universities. This initiative aims to foster greater research-industry collaboration and enhance the commercialisation of research outputs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing capital for start-ups, the fund is expected to enhance innovation and productivity through the commercialisation of research.
reforms.status_evidence: The CSIRO Innovation Fund was launched at the end of 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant initiative to enhance the start-up ecosystem in Australia, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: A new fund (the CSIRO Innovation Fund), announced as part of NISA, was launched at end-2016 by the government to support co-investment in new spin-off and start-up companies.

reforms.description: Australia is shifting from grant-based support to equity and debt support for green innovation through the establishment of the Clean Energy Innovation Fund, which will provide loans or take equity stakes in companies commercializing emerging technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the commercialization of innovative technologies, potentially leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have announced the establishment of the Clean Energy Innovation Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy for green innovation, which could have lasting impacts on the sector and the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Changes are underway to reduce grant-based support and increase equity and debt support for green innovation through a new fund, the Clean Energy Innovation Fund.

reforms.description: Australia's National Innovation and Science Agenda (NISA) aims to enhance the country's innovation capacity and productivity through targeted policy instruments that boost R&D activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing investment in R&D, the agenda is expected to drive productivity growth and innovation across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The NISA was officially announced to reform Australia's innovation landscape.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NISA represents a comprehensive approach to reforming Australia's innovation ecosystem, aiming for significant improvements in R&D investment and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Australia's National Innovation and Science Agenda (NISA) is adding fresh impetus to reform.

reforms.description: Australia's innovation policy aims to enhance productivity by fostering collaboration between universities and the business sector, particularly through commissioned and collaborative research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the transfer of knowledge from research institutions to businesses, enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy framework supports collaboration between research institutions and the business sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The policy enhances existing frameworks for collaboration, which is crucial for driving innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Research carried out by Australian universities and research institutes most directly impacts domestic productivity when these institutions work with the business sector.

reforms.description: The Australian government is focusing on ensuring that innovation policies are inclusive, particularly by supporting research that benefits low-to-middle income households.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By targeting support for innovations that benefit broader segments of society, the policy aims to enhance overall economic welfare.
reforms.status_evidence: Policy agendas already include measures to support innovations that can help address socio-economic gaps.
reforms.importance_rationale: The focus on inclusivity in innovation policy represents a significant shift towards addressing social equity through economic measures.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation policy can be inclusive in a broader sense by favouring innovation processes that bring benefits to the population at large.

reforms.description: The National Innovation and Science Agenda has proposed changes to corporate insolvency arrangements to better encourage risk-taking and entrepreneurship, including a safe harbour from personal liability for insolvent trading and making ipso facto clauses unenforceable during restructuring.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating easier restructuring and reducing the risks associated with insolvency, the reform encourages entrepreneurship and innovation, which can lead to productivity gains.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Innovation and Science Agenda has welcomed proposals to alter current corporate insolvency arrangements.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in insolvency policy aimed at fostering a more innovative and risk-taking business environment, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The National Innovation and Science Agenda has welcomed proposals to alter current corporate insolvency arrangements to better encourage risk taking and entrepreneurship.

reforms.description: Australia has introduced a Biomedical Translation Fund to facilitate the transition of firms from early to late-stage biomedical research, addressing the so-called 'valley of death' in funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted funding to bridge the gap between early and late-stage research, the fund enhances the commercialization potential of biomedical innovations, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund is being financed by redirecting initial capital contributions from the Medical Research Future Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant but targeted intervention to support the biomedical sector, which is crucial for innovation in health and medical research.
reforms.source_quote: This government-financed fund facilitates firms transition between early and late-stage biomedical research.

reforms.description: Australia's state-by-state reforms to accommodate ride-sharing services legalize these services and introduce regulatory standards, allowing them to compete with traditional taxi services on a more equal basis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms promote competition in the transport sector, potentially lowering prices and improving service quality for consumers.
reforms.status_evidence: Reforms have been finalized in New South Wales and the Australian Capital Territory to accommodate ride-sharing.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant adjustment to existing regulations, allowing for greater competition in the transport sector, which is economically relevant given the size of the market.
reforms.source_quote: States have been bringing in reforms that allow both traditional taxi services and ride-sharing to coexist.

reforms.description: The Australian authorities are maintaining an open approach to financial-sector disruption, including draft legislation allowing equity crowdfunding for companies, which aims to facilitate new business models.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enabling equity crowdfunding, the reform supports access to capital for startups, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.status_evidence: Recent policy measures included draft legislation allowing equity crowdfunding of companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of equity crowdfunding represents a significant change in the regulatory landscape for startups, enhancing their ability to raise funds.
reforms.source_quote: Recent policy measures included draft legislation allowing equity crowdfunding of companies.

reforms.description: Australia is implementing a multi-year reform to improve primary and secondary education, focusing on enhancing ICT and STEM skills to better prepare students for innovation-driven careers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and training enhances the skill set of the workforce, which is essential for fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Ongoing implementation of a multi-year reform to primary and secondary education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the education system aimed at enhancing the skill base necessary for innovation, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts to improve the system continue with ongoing implementation of a multi-year reform to primary and secondary education.

reforms.description: The Australian government is promoting public service innovation to enhance productivity and service quality, focusing on empowering the workforce and generating innovative ideas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging innovation in public services can lead to improved efficiency and effectiveness, contributing positively to overall economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have a welcomed degree of commitment to boosting innovation in the public sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing public sector productivity through innovation, which is crucial for improving service delivery and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have a welcomed degree of commitment to boosting innovation in the public sector.

reforms.description: The Business Research and Innovation Initiative was launched to invite SMEs to propose solutions to policy and service delivery challenges, with AUD 19 million allocated for grants to develop and test promising ideas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative supports SMEs in developing innovative solutions, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative was launched and grants were earmarked for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in fostering innovation among SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing overall economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: SMEs are being invited to propose solutions to five policy and service delivery challenges with AUD 19 million earmarked for grants to develop and test the most promising ideas.

reforms.description: A digital marketplace for IT procurement is being established to provide government agencies with an online directory of digital and technology services available from SME enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative enhances access to government contracts for SMEs, fostering competition and innovation in the public sector.
reforms.status_evidence: A digital marketplace is being established as part of the NISA initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a digital marketplace is a significant step towards modernizing public procurement processes and enhancing SME participation.
reforms.source_quote: A 'digital marketplace' for information technology procurement is being established to provide government agencies with an online directory of digital and technology services available from SME enterprises.

reforms.description: The establishment of a publicly funded digital research unit, Data61, aims to develop and apply new digital technologies for government and business by combining two existing bodies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to enhance Australia's digital capabilities, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The NISA proposes to establish a publicly funded digital research unit, Data61.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of Data61 represents a significant investment in digital research infrastructure, which is essential for fostering innovation in the digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The NISA proposes to establish a publicly funded digital research unit ('Data61') by combining two current bodies.

reforms.description: The Cooperative Research Centres programme has received over AUD 4 billion in government support, with additional contributions from stakeholders, aimed at fostering collaboration between research and industry sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between research and industry, leading to improved innovation outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been ongoing since the early 1990s and continues to operate with government support.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant structural initiative to enhance collaboration between research and industry, with substantial economic impacts.
reforms.source_quote: Since the Cooperative Research Centres programme began in the early 1990s, government has provided over AUD 4 billion in support.

reforms.description: The Australian government has introduced a new model for Research Block Grants (RBG) that combines existing schemes into two, focusing on research income from business and other end-users to enhance collaboration and commercialisation of research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing collaboration with industry, the reform is expected to enhance research outcomes and drive innovation, ultimately contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new arrangements began operating in early 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the funding system for university research, aiming to simplify processes and enhance collaboration, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A new model for Research Block Grants (RBG), announced as part of the recent National Innovation and Science Agenda (NISA), began operating in early 2017.

reforms.description: The ARC Linkage Projects scheme has transitioned to a continuous application process with fast-track decision-making to facilitate quicker collaboration between universities and businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining the application process, the reform is expected to enhance partnerships between academia and industry, leading to increased innovation and economic benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: The changes to the ARC Linkage Projects scheme have been put into effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly improves the efficiency of the ARC Linkage Projects scheme, enhancing collaboration potential but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The ARC Linkage Projects – designed to support research partnerships between universities and businesses – have moved to a continuous application and assessment process.

reforms.description: The Innovation Connections programme has been revamped to provide enhanced support for SMEs, including new grant components for researcher placements in businesses and vice versa.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating placements and connections between SMEs and research institutions, the programme is expected to enhance innovation capabilities within the SME sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The revamped Innovation Connections programme has been launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: The enhancements to the Innovation Connections programme provide significant support for SMEs, fostering innovation but do not constitute a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The new programme, Innovation Connections, expands the support for placements, notably of graduate or postgraduate researchers in businesses.

reforms.description: The CSIRO Innovation Fund was launched to support early-stage commercialization of research from CSIRO and other institutions, with a focus on co-investment in new spin-off and start-up companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance the commercialization of research, fostering innovation and productivity in the startup sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund was launched at the end of 2016 to support early-stage commercialization.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the CSIRO Innovation Fund represents a significant investment in the commercialization of research, which is crucial for enhancing Australia's innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: A new fund (the CSIRO Innovation Fund), announced as part of NISA, was launched at end-2016 by the government to support early stage commercialisation of research from CSIRO, other research institutions and universities.

reforms.description: The proposal for a nationally consistent approach to industry placements for higher degree research students aims to reduce complexity and enhance collaboration between universities and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating industry placements, the initiative aims to improve the skills and employability of researchers, thereby enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal for a nationally consistent approach is welcome.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant step towards improving the integration of research and industry, which is essential for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The proposal by the ACOLA review for a nationally consistent approach is welcome.

reforms.description: The Australian government is considering introducing an R&D tax-credit premium for business expenditure on collaborative research with publicly funded research organisations to enhance the effectiveness of the R&D Tax Incentive.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging collaboration between businesses and research institutions is expected to generate spillovers and enhance productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government could consider introducing an R&D tax-credit premium for business expenditure on collaborative research.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the existing R&D Tax Incentive by promoting collaboration, which is crucial for innovation and productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The government could consider introducing an R&D tax-credit premium for business expenditure on collaborative research with publicly funded research organisations.

reforms.description: The establishment of Innovation and Science Australia (ISA) aims to provide strategic whole-of-government advice on science, research, and innovation, enhancing coordination across various government departments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving coordination and strategic planning in science and innovation investments, ISA is expected to enhance the overall effectiveness of Australia's innovation system.
reforms.status_evidence: The relevant legislation for its establishment became effective in July 2016.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of ISA represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the coherence and effectiveness of Australia's innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: A new statutory independent board, Innovation and Science Australia (ISA), was announced in December 2015, as part of the NISA, with responsibility for 'strategic whole-of-government advice on all science, research and innovation matters'.

reforms.description: The establishment of Innovation and Science Australia (ISA) aims to create a coordinated approach to science, research, and innovation, enhancing collaboration between government, industry, and community sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration and coordination, ISA is expected to enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies and increase stakeholder support.
reforms.status_evidence: The establishment of ISA was officially announced as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of ISA represents a significant structural change in the governance of Australia's innovation system, aiming for a more integrated approach.
reforms.source_quote: ISA will also continue to perform the work of its predecessor, including the administration, monitoring, oversight and operation of programs such as the R&D Tax Incentive.

reforms.description: The government plans to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of the R&D Tax Incentive by adjusting its parameters and simplifying the application process.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By making the R&D Tax Incentive more effective and accessible, the reform is expected to stimulate private sector investment in research and development.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to enhance the R&D Tax Incentive.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the existing R&D Tax Incentive, aiming to improve its effectiveness and reduce administrative burdens.
reforms.source_quote: Enhancing the effectiveness and efficiency of the R&D Tax Incentive.
